verdict_reasoning: BAX is a canonically cytosolic/inactive pro-apoptotic Bcl-2 family protein that, upon apoptotic activation, oligomerizes and inserts into the mitochondrial outer membrane to drive MOMP. Critically, during apoptosis BAX has been documented on the plasma membrane outer leaflet (along with other cell-death-associated externalizations), making it accessible to surface-directed binders on dying cells. This places it in cell_state_induced (cell-death/ICD-related PM translocation). Rule-outs: no cancer ecto-pool independent of apoptosis as dominant story; no narrow germline/developmental lineage display; not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented steady-state PM cycling pool; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether documented apoptosis-associated BAX surface exposure is robust and targetable, vs. predominantly mitochondrial-outer-membrane insertion with minimal true PM outer-leaflet display.
